2026-01-12.seasonLabel: Monday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Let nothing disturb thee, nothing affright thee; all things are passing; God never changeth; patient endurance attaineth to all things; who God possesseth in nothing is wanting; alone God sufficeth.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Jesus (Ávila)

2026-01-13.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: At the close of life you will be examined as to your love.
quote.author: St. John of the Cross

2026-01-14.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: If you are what you ought to be, you will set fire to all Italy.
quote.author: St. Catherine of Siena

2026-01-15.seasonLabel: Thursday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I do not seek to understand that I may believe, but I believe in order to understand.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2026-01-16.seasonLabel: Friday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The world was not conceived by chance and without reason, but for an useful end and for the great advantage of all beings, since it is really the school where reasonable souls exercise themselves, the training ground where they learn to know God.
quote.author: St. Basil the Great

2026-01-17.seasonLabel: Saturday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: For that which He has not assumed He has not healed; but that which is united to His Godhead is also saved.
quote.author: St. Gregory Nazianzen

quote.text: The reason for loving God is God Himself; the measure, is to love Him without measure.
quote.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

2026-01-19.seasonLabel: Monday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: If thou wouldst know how these things are done, ask grace, not learning; desire, not intellect; the groaning of prayer, not the diligence of reading; the spouse, not the master; God, not man.
quote.author: St. Bonaventure

2026-01-20.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: He who prays is certainly saved; he who prays not is certainly damned.
quote.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori

2026-01-21.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Christ is the morning star, who promises and reveals to the saints the eternal light of life, when the night of the world is past.
quote.author: St. Bede the Venerable

2026-01-22.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Prayer is an uprising of the mind to God or a petitioning of God for what is fitting.
quote.author: St. John Damascene

2026-01-23.seasonLabel: Friday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Since then He Himself declared and said of the Bread, 'This is My Body,' who shall dare to doubt any longer?
quote.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem

2026-01-24.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I am the wheat of God, and let me be ground by the teeth of the wild beasts, that I may be found the pure bread of Christ.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

quote.text: For the glory of God is a living man; and the life of man consists in beholding God.
quote.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons

2026-01-26.seasonLabel: Monday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: He can no longer have God for his Father, who has not the Church for his mother.
quote.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage

2026-01-27.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Eighty and six years have I served Him, and He never did me any injury: how then can I blaspheme my King and my Saviour?
quote.author: St. Polycarp of Smyrna

2026-01-28.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time
2026-01-28.celebration: St. Thomas Aquinas, Priest and Doctor

quote.text: Grace does not destroy nature, but perfects it.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2026-01-29.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: What doth it profit thee to enter into deep discussion concerning the Holy Trinity, if thou lack humility, and be thus displeasing to the Trinity?
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2026-01-30.seasonLabel: Friday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Love is a great thing, a good above all others, which alone maketh every heavy burden light.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2026-01-31.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: God has created me to do Him some definite service; He has committed some work to me which He has not committed to another. I have my mission.
quote.author: St. John Henry Newman

quote.text: Christ be with me, Christ within me, Christ behind me, Christ before me, Christ beside me, Christ to win me, Christ to comfort and restore me.
quote.author: St. Patrick (attributed)

2026-02-02.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Hearken, O my son, to the precepts of thy master, and incline the ear of thine heart.
quote.author: St. Benedict of Nursia

2026-02-03.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Praised be my Lord God, with all His creatures, and especially our brother the Sun, who brings us the day, and who brings us the light.
quote.author: St. Francis of Assisi

2026-02-04.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Take, Lord, and receive all my liberty, my memory, my intellect, and all my will—all that I have and possess.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Loyola

2026-02-05.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The Christian ideal has not been tried and found wanting. It has been found difficult; and left untried.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

2026-02-06.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Love unites us to God; love covers a multitude of sins; love beareth all things, is long-suffering in all things.
quote.author: St. Clement of Rome

2026-02-07.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Before the blessing of the heavenly words another nature is spoken of, after the consecration the Body is signified.
quote.author: St. Ambrose of Milan

quote.text: Thou madest us for Thyself, and our heart is restless, until it repose in Thee.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-02-09.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Too late loved I Thee, O Thou Beauty of ancient days, yet ever new! too late I loved Thee!
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-02-10.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Grace does not destroy nature, but perfects it.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2026-02-11.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: In the heart of the Church, my Mother, I will be Love.
quote.author: St. Thérèse of the Child Jesus

2026-02-12.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: All true and living devotion presupposes the love of God; and indeed it is neither more nor less than a very real love of God.
quote.author: St. Francis de Sales

2026-02-13.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Would you do honour to Christ's body? Neglect Him not when naked; do not while here you honour Him with silken garments, neglect Him perishing without of cold and nakedness.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2026-02-14.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Ignorance of the Scriptures is ignorance of Christ.
quote.author: St. Jerome

quote.text: The divine words grow with the one who reads them.
quote.author: St. Gregory the Great

quote.text: Christian, acknowledge thy dignity, and becoming a partner in the Divine nature, refuse to return to the old baseness by degenerate conduct.
quote.author: St. Leo the Great

2027-01-11.seasonLabel: Monday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Let nothing disturb thee, nothing affright thee; all things are passing; God never changeth; patient endurance attaineth to all things; who God possesseth in nothing is wanting; alone God sufficeth.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Jesus (Ávila)

2027-01-12.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: At the close of life you will be examined as to your love.
quote.author: St. John of the Cross

2027-01-13.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: If you are what you ought to be, you will set fire to all Italy.
quote.author: St. Catherine of Siena

2027-01-14.seasonLabel: Thursday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I do not seek to understand that I may believe, but I believe in order to understand.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2027-01-15.seasonLabel: Friday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The world was not conceived by chance and without reason, but for an useful end and for the great advantage of all beings, since it is really the school where reasonable souls exercise themselves, the training ground where they learn to know God.
quote.author: St. Basil the Great

2027-01-16.seasonLabel: Saturday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: For that which He has not assumed He has not healed; but that which is united to His Godhead is also saved.
quote.author: St. Gregory Nazianzen

quote.text: The reason for loving God is God Himself; the measure, is to love Him without measure.
quote.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

2027-01-18.seasonLabel: Monday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: If thou wouldst know how these things are done, ask grace, not learning; desire, not intellect; the groaning of prayer, not the diligence of reading; the spouse, not the master; God, not man.
quote.author: St. Bonaventure

2027-01-19.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: He who prays is certainly saved; he who prays not is certainly damned.
quote.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori

2027-01-20.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Christ is the morning star, who promises and reveals to the saints the eternal light of life, when the night of the world is past.
quote.author: St. Bede the Venerable

2027-01-21.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Prayer is an uprising of the mind to God or a petitioning of God for what is fitting.
quote.author: St. John Damascene

2027-01-22.seasonLabel: Friday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Since then He Himself declared and said of the Bread, 'This is My Body,' who shall dare to doubt any longer?
quote.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem

2027-01-23.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I am the wheat of God, and let me be ground by the teeth of the wild beasts, that I may be found the pure bread of Christ.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

quote.text: For the glory of God is a living man; and the life of man consists in beholding God.
quote.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons

2027-01-25.seasonLabel: Monday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time
2027-01-25.celebration: The Conversion of St. Paul, Apostle

quote.text: He can no longer have God for his Father, who has not the Church for his mother.
quote.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage

2027-01-26.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Eighty and six years have I served Him, and He never did me any injury: how then can I blaspheme my King and my Saviour?
quote.author: St. Polycarp of Smyrna

2027-01-27.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: And on the day called Sunday, all who live in cities or in the country gather together to one place, and the memoirs of the apostles or the writings of the prophets are read.
quote.author: St. Justin Martyr

2027-01-28.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time
2027-01-28.celebration: St. Thomas Aquinas, Priest and Doctor

quote.text: Grace does not destroy nature, but perfects it.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2027-01-29.seasonLabel: Friday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Love is a great thing, a good above all others, which alone maketh every heavy burden light.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2027-01-30.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: God has created me to do Him some definite service; He has committed some work to me which He has not committed to another. I have my mission.
quote.author: St. John Henry Newman

quote.text: Christ be with me, Christ within me, Christ behind me, Christ before me, Christ beside me, Christ to win me, Christ to comfort and restore me.
quote.author: St. Patrick (attributed)

2027-02-01.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Hearken, O my son, to the precepts of thy master, and incline the ear of thine heart.
quote.author: St. Benedict of Nursia

2027-02-02.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Praised be my Lord God, with all His creatures, and especially our brother the Sun, who brings us the day, and who brings us the light.
quote.author: St. Francis of Assisi

2027-02-03.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Take, Lord, and receive all my liberty, my memory, my intellect, and all my will—all that I have and possess.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Loyola

2027-02-04.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The Christian ideal has not been tried and found wanting. It has been found difficult; and left untried.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

2027-02-05.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Love unites us to God; love covers a multitude of sins; love beareth all things, is long-suffering in all things.
quote.author: St. Clement of Rome

2027-02-06.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Before the blessing of the heavenly words another nature is spoken of, after the consecration the Body is signified.
quote.author: St. Ambrose of Milan

quote.text: Thou madest us for Thyself, and our heart is restless, until it repose in Thee.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2027-02-08.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Too late loved I Thee, O Thou Beauty of ancient days, yet ever new! too late I loved Thee!
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2027-02-09.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Grace does not destroy nature, but perfects it.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

quote.text: Yesterday I was crucified with Him; today I am glorified with Him. Yesterday I died with Him; today I am quickened with Him.

2027-03-29.seasonLabel: Monday within the Octave of Easter

quote.text: This is the Halleluia which we sing, which, as you know, meaneth, Praise ye the Lord. Therefore that period is before the Lord's Resurrection, this, after His Resurrection: by which time is signified the future hope which as yet we have not: for what we represent after the Lord's Resurrection, we shall have after our own.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
